title: Akt administracyjny – wczoraj i dziś (rozważania na kanwie przemyśleń Stanisława Kasznicy)
author: Kmieciak, Zbigniew
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: At the end of the first half of the last century, a work by Stanisław Kasznica: Polish Administrative Law was published. The basic concepts and institutions can be regarded as a synthesis of the achievements of the then scientific thought, and at the same time a "signpost" indicating the directions of research. In the conditions of shaping the foundations of socialism, it was a serious challenge for at least part of the scientific community. It faced a dilemma: whether and to what extent the concepts developed in legal systems that respect the universal ideas of liberalism and the Rule of Law can be adapted to a different political and systemic reality. Life has givean a clear answer to this question. What is a product of the legal culture of many nations and the truth about it has survived and even created strong foundations for the further development of the science of public law in Poland. The concept of an administrative act presented by the author has of course undergone many modifications resulting from the transformation of the administration itself, technological progress and new challenges for the state and its services. To this day, however, it is a source of inspiration and a point of reference for research.
